StudentProfile.title: StudentProfile
StudentProfile.description: Represents a student's profile within the aviation academy.

id.description: Unique identifier for the StudentProfile entity.

trainingProgress.description: Current training progress as a percentage or other numerical representation.

flightHours.description: Total flight hours accumulated by the student.

Instructor.title: Instructor
Instructor.description: Represents an instructor at the aviation academy.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Instructor entity.

Aircraft.title: Aircraft
Aircraft.description: Represents an aircraft used for training.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Aircraft entity.

maintenanceLogs.description: References to MaintenanceLog entries. (Relationship: Aircraft 1:N MaintenanceLog)

MaintenanceLog.title: MaintenanceLog
MaintenanceLog.description: Represents a maintenance log entry for an aircraft.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MaintenanceLog entity.

procedure.description: Details of the maintenance procedure followed.

Personnel.title: Personnel
Personnel.description: Represents a person working at the aviation academy.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Personnel entity.
Unique identifier for the SafetyProtocol entity.

description.description: Detailed description of the safety protocol.

complianceChecks.description: References to ComplianceCheck entries. (Relationship: SafetyProtocol 1:N ComplianceCheck)

id.description: Unique identifier for the ComplianceCheck entity.

date.description: Date when the compliance check was performed.

result.description: Result of the compliance check (true for pass, false for fail).

notes.description: Additional notes or observations from the compliance check.

Permission.title: Permission
Permission.description: Represents a permission within the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Permission entity.

description.description: Description of what the permission allows.

Role.title: Role
Role.description: Represents a role within the application, defining a set of permissions.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Role entity.

permissions.description: References to Permissions. (Relationship: Role N:N Permission)

Company.title: Company
Company.description: Represents a Company on the system.

Settings.title: Settings
Settings.description: Represents the application Settings for a company.

companyId.description: The company this setting belongs to.

definition.description: Stores company information. All other collections except users are company specific.
Stores user profile information. All other user specific data is stored under the company id.
Stores student profile information, scoped to a specific company.

params.name: studentProfileId
params.description: Unique identifier for the student profile.

definition.description: Stores instructor information, scoped to a specific company.
Stores aircraft information, scoped to a specific company.
Stores maintenance log entries for a specific aircraft, scoped to a specific company.

params.name: maintenanceLogId
params.description: Unique identifier for the maintenance log entry.

definition.description: Stores personnel information, scoped to a specific company.
Stores safety protocols, scoped to a specific company.

params.name: safetyProtocolId
params.description: Unique identifier for the safety protocol.

definition.description: Stores compliance checks for a specific safety protocol, scoped to a specific company.

params.name: safetyProtocolId
params.description: Unique identifier for the safety protocol.

params.name: complianceCheckId
params.description: Unique identifier for the compliance check.

definition.description: Stores roles, scoped to a specific company.
Stores the application settings for a company.

params.name: settingsId
params.description: Unique identifier for the settings document.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to support a multi-company aviation academy management application, focusing on security, scalability, and ease of debugging. Authorization Independence is achieved through denormalization where needed, especially for collaborative data. Structural Segregation ensures that each collection has a homogeneous security posture. Access Modeling follows consistent patterns, utilizing path-based ownership for user-specific data and membership maps for collaborative entities.  The structure is multi-company, where companies are isolated from each other. The structure uses the companies collection to keep a list of companies and provides a company id for all other collections in the database. This strategy allows the application to manage multiple academies within the same system, each with its own set of data and settings.

*   **Companies:** The root level collection `companies` stores individual company documents. Each company has its own unique ID and associated settings.
*   **Users:** User data is stored in a top-level collection called `users` and is independent from the company id. It contains basic authentication information.
*   **User Profiles:** The `StudentProfile` and `Instructor` entities are stored as subcollections under each `company`. This isolates data between companies. A similar strategy is used for other entities.

QAPs (Rules are not Filters) is addressed by:

*   Segregating data into collections with homogeneous security requirements.
*   Using path-based ownership for private data.
*   Employing membership maps for collaborative data, and storing these maps directly within the documents to avoid `get()` calls in security rules.

Specifically, the following denormalization strategies are used:

*   Authorization context (e.g., members, ownerId) is copied into subcollection documents to avoid hierarchical dependencies.
*   Company ID is included in all documents except for users to support multi-tenancy access control.

This design enables robust security rules that are simple to write, easy to understand, and highly performant.
